evals.name: hook-prettier-onsave
evals.prompt:
I have a tiny shell script that runs prettier across the repo whenever I save a file, and I want to publish it as a hook in my team's maconfai distribution repo (we already have a `.git/` and a `README.md` at the root of the repo I'm sending you — treat the current working directory as that repo root). Here is the script content:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
npx --yes prettier --write . --log-level=warn || true
```

Add this as a hook named `prettier-on-save`. It should fire after a file is edited by the agent. I need it to work for both Claude Code users and Cursor users on my team. Don't forget to update the README so my teammates can see it's available.
evals.expected_output: Creates hooks/prettier-on-save/hooks.json with both claude-code and cursor event handlers, hooks/prettier-on-save/<script.sh> (executable, contains the prettier command), and updates README.md to list the hook. Paths inside hooks.json must reference .agents/hooks/prettier-on-save/<script>.

evals.name: mcp-github
evals.prompt: Add a GitHub MCP server to this maconfai distribution repo (treat the current working directory as the repo root, README.md is already there). Use the official `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` package, run it via npx, and it needs a `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var. I want it to work for all the agents maconfai supports — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Open Code — without me writing per-agent configs by hand. Also list it in the README.
evals.expected_output: Creates mcps/github/mcp.json with the canonical {"mcpServers": {"github": {...}}} shape using `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github` and env `GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"` (bare syntax, NOT ${env:GITHUB_TOKEN}). README updated. No per-agent files written manually.

evals.name: skill-pr-reviewer
evals.prompt: Create a new skill in this maconfai distribution repo (cwd = repo root) called `pr-reviewer`. The skill should help the agent review a pull request: read the diff, identify risky changes (auth, migrations, anything touching `.env` or secrets), suggest tests where coverage is thin, and post structured feedback (summary + per-file comments). I want my teammates to be able to install it with `npx maconfai install owner/repo` and have it auto-trigger when they ask the agent to review a PR. Don't forget the README.
evals.expected_output: Creates skills/pr-reviewer/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (`name: pr-reviewer`, descriptive `description` that mentions PR review triggers), body covering the requested behaviors. README updated. No extraneous files.
